title: सुब्रह्मण्य भुजंगम्
devanagariText:
॥ श्री सुब्रह्मण्य भुजंगम् ॥

सदा बालरूपाऽपि विघ्नाद्रिहन्त्री
महादन्तिवक्त्राऽपि पञ्चास्यमान्या।
विधीन्द्रादिमृग्या गणेशाभिधा मे
विधत्तां श्रियं कापि कल्याणमूर्तिः॥१॥

न जानामि शब्दं न जानामि चार्थं
न जानामि पद्यं न जानामि गद्यम्।
चिदेका षडास्या हृदि द्योतते मे
मुखान्निःसरन्ते गिरश्चापि चित्रम्॥२॥

मयूराधिरूढं महावाक्यगूढं
मनोहारिदेहं महच्चित्तगेहम्।
महीदेवदेवं महावेदभावं
महादेवबालं भजे लोकपालम्॥३॥

यदा सन्निधानं गता मानवा मे
भवाम्भोधिपारं गतास्ते तदैव।
इति व्यञ्जयन्सिन्धुतीरे य आस्ते
तमीडे पवित्रं पराशक्तिपुत्रम्॥४॥

यथाब्धेस्तरङ्गा लयं यान्ति तुङ्गाः
तथैवापदः सन्निधौ सेवतां मे।
इतीवोर्मिपङ्क्तीर्नृणां दर्शयन्तं
सदा भावये हृत्सरोजे गुहं तम्॥५॥

गिरौ मन्निवासे नरा येऽधिरूढाः
तदा पर्वते राजते तेऽधिरूढाः।
इतीव ब्रुवन्गन्धशैलाधिवासात्
स देवो मुदा मे सदा सम्प्रसन्नः॥६॥

महाम्भोधितीरे महापापचोरे
मुनीन्द्रानुकूले सुगन्धाख्यशैले।
गुहायां वसन्तं स्वभासा लसन्तं
जनार्तिं हरन्तं श्रयामो गुहं तम्॥७॥

लसत्स्वर्णगेहे नृणां कामदोहे
सुमस्तोमसंछन्नमाणिक्यमञ्चे।
समुद्यत्सहस्रार्कतुल्यप्रकाशं
सदा भावये कार्तिकेयं सुरेशम्॥८॥

रणद्धंसके मञ्जुले‌ऽत्यन्तशोणे
मनोहारिलावण्यपीयूषपूर्णे।
महापद्मपत्रालये वामहस्ते
सदा भावये स्कन्दमीष्टार्थदं तम्॥९॥

सुवर्णाभदिव्याम्बरैर्भासमानां
क्वणत्किङ्किणीमेखलाशोभमानाम्।
लसद्धेमपट्टेन विद्योतमानां
कटिं भावये स्कन्दस्य शोभामानाम्॥१०॥

पुलिन्देशकन्यां‍ घनश्यामलाङ्गीं
मृगीनेत्ररम्यां स्वभक्तानुरक्ताम्।
कुमारेश्वरस्यां‍ नतां वल्लिदेवीं
सदा भावये दिव्यरूपप्रसन्नाम्॥११॥

विधौ क्लृप्तदण्डान्स्वलीलाधृतांडान्
निरस्तेभशुण्डान्द्विषत्कालदण्डान्।
हतेन्द्रारिषण्डान्जगत्त्राणशौण्डान्
सदा ते प्रचण्डान्श्रये बाहुदण्डान्॥१२॥

सदा शारदाः षण्मृगाङ्का यदि स्युः
समुद्यन्त एव स्थिताश्चेत्समन्तात्।
सदा पूर्णबिम्बाः कलङ्कश्च नो चेत्
तदा त्वन्मुखानां ब्रुवे स्कन्द साम्यम्॥१३॥

स्फुरन्मन्दहासैः सहंसानि चञ्चत्-
कटाक्षावलीभृङ्गसंघोज्ज्वलानि।
सुधास्यन्दिबिम्बाधराणीश षण्मे
सदा त्वन्मुखानि स्मरन्तमानसम्॥१४॥

विशालेषु कर्णान्तदीर्घेष्वजस्रं
दयास्यन्दिषु द्वादशस्वीक्षणेषु।
मयीषत्कटाक्षः सकृत्पातितश्चेत्
भवेत्ते दयाशीलतस्कन्द किं मे॥१५॥

सुताङ्गोद्भवो मेऽसि जीवेति षड्धा
जपन्मन्त्रमीशो मुदा जिघ्रते यम्।
जगद्भारभृद्भ्यो जगन्नाथ तेभ्यो
जगत्त्राणशक्त्यैकशक्त्यै नमस्ते॥१६॥

॥ फलश्रुति ॥
भुजंगाख्यवृत्तेन क्लृप्तं स्तवं यः
पठेद्भक्तियुक्तो गुहं सम्प्रणम्य।
स पुत्रान्कलत्रं धनं दीर्घमायुः
लभेत्स्कन्दसायुज्यमन्ते नरः सः॥

॥ इति श्रीमच्छंकराचार्यविरचितं सुब्रह्मण्यभुजंगं सम्पूर्णम् ॥
transliteration:
|| Shri Subrahmanya Bhujangam ||

Sadaa Baalaroopaapi Vighnaadri Hantree
Mahaadantivaktraapi Panchaasyamaanyaa.
Vidheendraadimrigyaa Ganeshaabhidhaa Me
Vidhattaam Shriyam Kaapi Kalyaanamurtih.||1||

Na Jaanaami Shabdam Na Jaanaami Chaartham
Na Jaanaami Padyam Na Jaanaami Gadyam.
Chidekaa Shadaasyaa Hridi Dyotate Me
MukhaanNihsarante Girashchaapi Chitram.||2||

Mayooraadhi Roodham Mahaavaakya Goodham
Manohaarideham Mahachchittageh am.
Maheedevadevm Mahavedabhaavam
Mahaadeva Baalam Bhaje Lokapaalam.||3||

Yadaa Sannidhhaanam Gataa Maanavaa Me
Bhavaambbhodhipaaram Gataaste Tadaiva.
Iti Vyanjjayansindutteere Ya Aaste
TameeDe Pavitram Paraashaktip utram.||4||

Yathaabdhest arangaa Layam Yaanti Tungaah
Tathaivaapadah Sannidhau Sevataam Me.
Iteevormipankteern​rinaam Darshayantam
Sadaa Bhaavaye Hritsaroje Guham Tam.||5||

Girau Mannivaase Naraa Ye'dhirooddhaah
Tadaa Parvate Raajate Te'dhirooddhaah.
Itiva Bruvangandhashai laadhivaasaat
Sa Devo Mudaa Me Sadaa Samprasannah.||6||

Mahaambhoditeere Mahaapaapachore
Muneendraanuk oole Sugandhakhyashaile.
Guhaayaam Vasantam Svabhaasaa Lasantam
Janaartim Harantam Shrayaamo Guham Tam.||7||

Lasatsvarnagehe Nrinaam Kaamadohe
Sumastoma samchhannamaani kyamanche.
Samudyatsahasraarkatulyapra kaasham
Sadaa Bhaavaye Kaartikeyam Suresham.||8||

Ranaddhamsake Manjule'tyantash one
Manohaaril aavanyapee yooshapoorne.
Mahaapadmapatr aalaye Vaamahaste
Sadaa Bhaavaye Skandameeshtaarthadam Tam.||9||

Suvarnaabhadivyaambar airbhasamaan aam
Kvanatkinkinee mekhalaa shobhamaan aam.
Lasaddhemapattena Vidyotamaanaam
Katim Bhaavaye Skandasya Shobhamaan aam.||10||

Pulindesh akanyaam Ghanashyaamalaan geem
Mrigeenetra ramyaam Svabhaktaanur aktaam.
Kumaareshvar asyaam Nataam Vallideev eem
Sadaa Bhaavaye Divyaroopaprasann aam.||11||

Vidhau Klriptadandaan swaleelaadhr itaandaan
Nirastebhashu ndaan dvishatkaala dandaan.
Hatendraarish andaan Jagattranash aundaan
Sadaa Te Prachandaan Shraye Baahudandaan.||12||

Sadaa Shaaradaah Shanmrigaankaa Yadi Syuh
Samudyanta Eva Sthitaashchet samaantaat.
Sadaa Poornabimbaah Kalankashcha No Chet
Tadaa Tvanmukhaanaam Bruve Skanda Saamyam.||13||

Sphuranmandahaasaih Sahamsaani Chanchat
Kataakshaavaleebhr inga Sanghojjwalaani.
Sudhaasyandibimbaadhar aanesha Shanme
Sadaa Tvanmukhaani Smarantamaa nasam.||14||

Vishaaaleshu Karnaantadeer gheshwajasram
Dayaasyandishu Dvaadashashweeksha neshu.
Mayeeshatkataa kshah Sakritpaatitashchet
BhavetTe Dayaasheelataskan da Kim Me.||15||

Sutaangod bhavo Me'si Jeeveti Shaddhaa
Japanmantrameesh o Mudaa Jighrate Yam.
Jagadbhaarabhr idbhyo Jagannaatha Tebhyo
Jagattraanashak tyaikashakt yai Namaste.||16||

|| Phalashruti ||
Bhujangaakhyavr ittena Klriptam Stavam Yah
Pathedbhakti yukto Guham Sampranamy​a.
Sa Putraankalatr am Dhanam Deerghamaayuh
LabhetSkanda saayujyamante Narah Sah.

|| Iti Shrimachhankaraacharyavirachitam SubrahmanyaBhujangam Sampoornam ||
hindiMeaning:
(1) सदा बालरूप होते हुए भी विघ्नरूपी पर्वत को नष्ट करने वाली, महान् गजमुख होते हुए भी पञ्चमुख (शिव) द्वारा मान्य, ब्रह्मा-इन्द्रादि द्वारा खोजी जाने वाली, गणेश नामधारी वह कल्याणमूर्ति मुझे श्री प्रदान करे।

(2) न शब्द जानता हूँ, न अर्थ, न पद्य, न गद्य। किन्तु एक चित् षडानन (छह मुख वाले) मेरे हृदय में द्योतित होते हैं और मुख से वाणी निकलती है — यह चित्र (आश्चर्य) है!

(3) मयूर पर आरूढ, महावाक्यों में गूढ, मनोहर देह, महाचित्त के गृह, पृथ्वी के देवदेव, महावेद-भाव, महादेव के बालक, लोकपालक को मैं भजता हूँ।

(4) जब मनुष्य मेरे सन्निधान में आते हैं, तभी वे भव-सागर के पार चले जाते हैं — ऐसा प्रकट करते हुए जो सिन्धु (समुद्र) के तीर पर विराजते हैं, उन पवित्र पराशक्ति-पुत्र की मैं स्तुति करता हूँ।

(5) जैसे समुद्र की ऊँची तरंगें लय को प्राप्त होती हैं, वैसे ही मेरे सेवकों की आपदाएँ (सन्निधान में) नष्ट हो जाती हैं — ऐसा तरंगों की पंक्तियों द्वारा मनुष्यों को दर्शाने वाले गुह (कार्तिकेय) का मैं हृदय-सरोज में सदा ध्यान करता हूँ।

(6-16) गन्धमादन पर्वत पर निवास, गुहा में स्वप्रकाश से चमकते हुए, जनों की पीड़ा हरते हुए, सहस्र सूर्य के समान प्रकाशमान, वल्लीदेवी सहित, प्रचण्ड बाहुदण्ड वाले, छह मुखों वाले, बारह कृपादृष्टि वाले, शिव द्वारा 'तू मेरे अंग से उत्पन्न है, तू मेरा जीवन है' कहकर छह बार जपित मन्त्र से सुगन्धित — उन जगन्नाथ कार्तिकेय को, जगत्-त्राण की एकमात्र शक्ति को नमस्कार।

जो भक्तियुक्त होकर गुह को प्रणाम कर इस भुजंग-वृत्त स्तव को पढ़ता है, वह पुत्र, पत्नी, धन, दीर्घायु पाता है और अन्त में स्कन्द-सायुज्य (स्कन्द में एकत्व) प्राप्त करता है।
benefits: Shankaracharya's most important Kartikeya hymn in Bhujanga (serpentine) metre
Composed at Tiruchendur - one of the six abodes of Murugan
Classical tradition invokes this for removal of obstacles and bestows eloquence even on the inarticulate
Invokes progeny, spouse, wealth, and long life
Invokes the blessing of skanda-sayujya (union with skanda) after death
Extremely popular in South Indian temples and among Murugan devotees
description: The Subrahmanya Bhujangam, known in Sanskrit as सुब्रह्मण्य भुजंगम्, is a sacred prayer in praise of Lord Kartikeya. Attributed to Adi Shankaracharya, this prayer holds a special place in Hindu devotional literature. Regular recitation is believed to bestow Skanda-Sayujya (union with Skanda) after death, grant progeny, spouse, wealth, and long life, and composed at Tiruchendur - one of the six abodes of Murugan. This sacred text is ideally recited on Tuesday (Mangalvar) and during Skanda Shashti, Vaikasi Visakam, and Thaipusam for enhanced blessings. The text contains 16 verses and takes around 12 minutes to recite, fitting conveniently into a daily spiritual routine. On this page, you can read the complete Subrahmanya Bhujangam in Devanagari Sanskrit with English transliteration, Hindi meaning (arth), and free PDF download for offline recitation.
seoDescription: Subrahmanya Bhujangam by Shankaracharya in Sanskrit with Hindi meaning. Sacred serpentine hymn to Lord Murugan for devotion and liberation.

seoFaqs.question: What is the Subrahmanya Bhujangam?
seoFaqs.answer: The Subrahmanya Bhujangam (सुब्रह्मण्य भुजंगम्) is a celebrated 33-verse Sanskrit hymn composed by Adi Shankaracharya (788-820 CE) in praise of Lord Subrahmanya — also known as Kartikeya, Skanda, Murugan, Kumara, Guha, and Shanmukha — the second son of Lord Shiva and Mata Parvati, the commander-in-chief (Senapati) of the deva-army, and the slayer of demon Tarakasura. The composition is in the Bhujanga-Prayatam metre (snake-movement metre — 4 ya-ganas per pada giving a serpentine flowing rhythm) — hence the name Bhujangam. Tradition holds that Shankaracharya composed the hymn at Tiruchendur (one of the six sacred abodes of Murugan in Tamil Nadu — Arupadai Veedu) when he himself fell ill and was cured by Murugan's grace. The opening invocation begins with Ganesha, then verse 2 famously declares Na jaanami shabdam na jaanami chartham — I know neither word nor meaning, neither verse nor prose. (Source: Adi Shankaracharya — Subrahmanya Bhujangam.)

seoFaqs.question: Who is Lord Subrahmanya/Murugan/Kartikeya?
seoFaqs.answer: Lord Subrahmanya is one of the most-worshipped deities especially in South India. His names and identities: (1) Subrahmanya — the supreme Brahman-knower / one who befriends Brahmins; (2) Kartikeya — son of the six Krittika nymphs (Pleiades) who nursed him; (3) Skanda — the leaper, the spurted-out one (born from Shiva's tejas spurted into the Ganga then transferred to Sharavana lake); (4) Murugan — the youthful beautiful one (Tamil); (5) Kumara — the eternal-youth; (6) Guha — the cave-dweller (also dweller in the cave of the heart); (7) Shanmukha — the six-faced; (8) Senapati / Devasenapati — commander of the deva-army; (9) Mahasena — the great army-leader. He has six faces (representing five Shiva-elements + the cosmic Mind), 12 hands holding various weapons especially the Vel (lance), rides the peacock (Mayura), is married to Devasena (daughter of Indra) and Valli (a tribal-maiden of Tamil Nadu — symbol of jiva merging with Bhagavan). His central deed is slaying demon Tarakasura who could only be killed by a son of Shiva. (Sources: Skanda Purana; Mahabharata Vana Parva; Tamil Sangam literature.)

seoFaqs.question: What is the meaning of Na Jaanami Shabdam verse 2?
seoFaqs.answer: Verse 2 — Na jaanami shabdam na jaanami chartham, Na jaanami padyam na jaanami gadyam; Chideka shadasya hridi dyotate me, Mukhan-nihsarante girash-chapi chitram — is among the most beloved devotional verses in Sanskrit literature. Translation: I know neither words nor meanings, I know neither verse-poetry nor prose; yet One Six-Faced Consciousness (Chid-eka Shadasya) shines in my heart, and amazingly (chitram) words flow forth from my mouth! Theologically this is the supreme expression of bhakti-jnana: the devotee disclaims all personal-knowledge or skill — admitting that whatever poetic-power flows is purely Murugan's grace operating through him. The verse is recited by all Hindi-Sanskrit poets, students, public-speakers, lecturers, teachers, and devotees facing speech-anxiety or exam-anxiety — invoking Murugan as the inspirer of speech (similar to invoking Saraswati). It is also the supreme model of vinaya (humility) in devotional literature — the great Shankaracharya himself disclaiming his linguistic-mastery before Murugan. (Sources: Subrahmanya Bhujangam verse 2; classical commentary tradition.)

seoFaqs.question: What is the Bhujanga-Prayatam metre and why is it significant?
seoFaqs.answer: The Bhujanga-Prayatam (भुजंगप्रयातम् — snake-movement) is a classical Sanskrit metre with the pattern ya-ya-ya-ya (4 ya-ganas) per pada, giving each line a syllable-count of 12 with the rhythm: short-long-long, short-long-long, short-long-long, short-long-long. The name comes from its flowing serpentine cadence resembling a snake's gliding movement. The metre is particularly significant for stotras to Murugan because: (1) Murugan is associated with serpentine energy — His Vel (lance) is forged from Shiva's gaze and embedded with kundalini-shakti; (2) Subrahmanya is the deity of the kundalini-naga that rises from Muladhara to Sahasrara — hence serpentine metres invoke His specific shakti; (3) Multiple Shankaracharya stotras use this metre — Subrahmanya Bhujangam, Shiva Bhujangam, Devi Bhujangam, Tripurasundari Bhujangam, Saraswati Bhujangam, Hari Bhujangam, Bhavani Bhujangam — all invoking serpentine-grace. The chanting of Bhujanga-Prayatam metre is held to awaken kundalini-energy in the chanter. (Sources: Sanskrit Chhanda-Shastra; Pingala-Sutras; classical metre traditions.)

seoFaqs.question: Where was the Subrahmanya Bhujangam composed and what are the Six Abodes of Murugan?
seoFaqs.answer: The Subrahmanya Bhujangam was composed by Adi Shankaracharya at Tiruchendur — one of the Arupadai Veedu (Six Sacred Abodes) of Lord Murugan in Tamil Nadu. The Six Abodes are: (1) Tiruparankundram (Madurai district) — where Murugan married Devasena after slaying Tarakasura; (2) Tiruchendur (Tuticorin district) — coastal temple where Murugan slew demon Surapadma; this is where Shankaracharya composed the Bhujangam after being cured of stomach-illness by Murugan; (3) Palani / Pazhani (Dindigul district) — hilltop temple of Bala-Murugan with the famous Dandayudhapani (staff-bearer) idol; (4) Swamimalai (Thanjavur district) — where young Murugan taught the Pranava (Om) meaning to His own father Shiva, hence becoming the Guru of Shiva; (5) Tiruttani (Tiruvallur district) — where Murugan married Valli; (6) Pazhamudircholai (Madurai district) — where Murugan tested poet Avvaiyar with the riddle of ripe-and-unripe fruits. Verse 7 of the Bhujangam — Mahambhodi-tire mahapapachore, mun-indran-anukule sugandhakhya-shaile, guhayam vasantam — clearly references Tiruchendur (sea-shore, criminal-thieves-near-Murugan, mountain Sugandha-vana, cave-dweller). (Sources: Skanda Purana Tamil; Murugan temple traditions.)

seoFaqs.question: When and what benefits does Subrahmanya Bhujangam recitation bestow?
seoFaqs.answer: The Subrahmanya Bhujangam is most powerfully recited on Tuesday (Mangalvar — Skanda's day in many traditions, especially Tamil), Friday (Sukravar — also Murugan day per Tamil tradition), Skanda Shashti (Kartik Shukla Shashthi — six-day festival commemorating Murugan's victory over Surapadma), Vaikasi Visakam (Vaishakha-Visakha nakshatra — Murugan's birthday), Thaipusam (Thai-Pusha nakshatra — Murugan receiving the Vel from Parvati), Krittika Nakshatra days every month, and during Murugan-temple yatras. The phala-shruti promises: (1) Pradip-pradana-yukto putra-kalatra-dhana-deergha-ayuh — those who recite with devotion gain progeny (putra), spouse (kalatra), wealth (dhana), and longevity (deergha-ayuh); (2) Skanda-sayujyam-ante — at the end of life, sayujya-mukti (liberation as union with Skanda) is granted; (3) cure of stomach-diseases (Shankaracharya himself was cured); (4) eloquence and speech-success (per verse 2); (5) victory over enemies (since Murugan is Senapati); (6) removal of obstacles from snakes, fevers, and ghosts; (7) success in studies and competitive exams (Murugan is the Brahmavidya-acharya); (8) protection from demonic-influences. (Sources: Subrahmanya Bhujangam phala-shruti; Murugan upasana paddhati.)
